g.text: Credential Revocation Lifecycle - Admin-initiated and Holder-requested (two cases, shared mechanism)
Status List hosted by Core at /ssi/revocation/v1/list/{id}
[SPEC] TWO initiation paths in this file
CASE A - Issuer Admin initiates the action directly
CASE B - Holder REQUESTS revocation -> Issuer approves -> revoke
Both converge on the SHARED mechanism (Core changes status + re-publishes Status List)
Principle: Holder may REQUEST ; only the Issuer can REVOKE (W3C VC 2.0)
CASE A : ADMIN / ISSUER INITIATES DIRECTLY
permission to manage this credential type / organisation
REVOKE: from VALID/SUSPENDED , SUSPEND: from VALID , REACTIVATE: from SUSPENDED
decidedAction set -> proceed to SHARED below
[CASE B : Holder requests revocation (request -> issuer approval)]
CASE B : HOLDER REQUESTS -> ISSUER APPROVES
B1. Holder submits a request (typed reason)
credentialId , reasonType (LOST / STOLEN / COMPROMISED / NO_LONGER_NEEDED / DATA_ERROR) , reasonNote
not already REVOKED , no PENDING request
failureStep = VALIDATE_REQUEST , reason = already revoked / duplicate pending
Reject - already revoked or request pending
status = REQUESTED , reasonType , reasonNote , requestedAt , holderId
[SPEC] Fast-path: contain immediately, confirm later (reversible if false report)
Core re-signs + re-publishes Status List (see SHARED)
Acknowledged - credential SUSPENDED immediately , under review
B3. Issuer reviews within SLA (PDPA timeframe)
[SPEC] SLA (PDPA): decide within the response window ; else escalation / default policy
Review request (may re-verify holder identity / loss report)
Notify - request REJECTED + reason (may re-request later)
decidedAction = REVOKE -> proceed to SHARED below
SHARED : CORE APPLIES STATUS CHANGE + PUBLISH STATUS LIST
[An action was decided (Case A: transition allowed OR Case B: issuer approved)]
SHARED. Core applies the status change + publishes Status List
[SPEC] Reached ONLY when an action was decided
Reject / invalid paths above end without reaching here
Set / Clear status bit at statusListIndex
bitstring -> gzip + base64 (reflecting the new bit)
New status (REVOKED / SUSPENDED / VALID) , statusListIndex , effectiveAt
credentialId , status , reason , effectiveAt , suspendEndDate (if SUSPEND) , actionBy
credentialId , reason , actionBy , effectiveAt , auditHash , auditSignature , previousAuditHash , retentionUntil , immutableStorage = WORM
status = newStatus (do not change holder key / reference)
[No action - request was invalid or rejected (flow already ended above)]
Next VP verification fetches Status List -> REVOKED / SUSPENDED -> verification FAILS
-> VALID (after reactivate) -> verification can pass again
If SUSPEND had suspendEndDate, a scheduler reactivates at that time (re-runs REACTIVATE)
Holder right to request -> NIST 800-63B (invalidation) , PDPA s.19/33 , eIDAS user control
Issuer authority to decide -> W3C VC 2.0 (issuer controls status) , ETDA Trust Framework
SLA + mandatory reject reason -> PDPA / GDPR
Status list mechanism -> W3C Bitstring Status List (see Lifecycle-2)
Audit at every step -> PDPA s.39 accountability , ISO 27001
